diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dactylitis (uniform swelling of the whole digit from tenosynovial and entheseal inflammation) is a hallmark of psoriatic/seronegative spondyloarthritis.
Gout typically produces focal monoarticular inflammation (e.g. podagra), not uniform whole-digit dactylitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nail pitting on the affected hand

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nail pitting/dystrophy reflects shared nail-bed and entheseal involvement and is strongly associated with psoriatic arthritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Simultaneous asymmetrical involvement of two distinct joint types (small digit + large knee, opposite sides)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An asymmetrical oligoarticular pattern spanning a small and a large joint is characteristic of psoriatic arthritis.
Septic arthritis is usually monoarticular; simultaneous involvement of two distinct joints on opposite sides is atypical for a single septic joint.

symptoms.symptom_name: Established psoriasis (flaky skin patches for years)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A background of cutaneous psoriasis is the defining substrate for psoriatic arthritis.
An established psoriatic skin history points to psoriatic rather than reactive arthritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No high spiking fever, no rigors, no tachycardia/breathlessness (no systemic toxicity)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a high swinging fever, rigors or systemic toxicity lowers (but does not exclude) the probability of a septic joint / bacteraemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Warm swollen knee but skin not spreading fiery-red and not worsening hour-by-hour

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A warm effusion that is not spreading-erythematous or rapidly progressive fits an inflammatory flare.
Warmth alone cannot exclude sepsis remotely; the absence of spreading erythema and rapid hourly progression is reassuring but joint aspiration remains the discriminator.

symptoms.symptom_name: Constitutional fatigue preceding the flare; paracetamol ineffective

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A constitutional prodrome and poor response to simple analgesia are consistent with a systemic inflammatory flare rather than a mechanical problem.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lives alone; dominant hand and weight-bearing knee both disabled (self-care risk)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Functional impact does not change the diagnosis but is central to the disposition and support plan.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs: BP 130/80, HR 88, Temp 37.3 C, RR 16, SpO2 99% (no systemic sepsis physiology)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician measurement: only a low-grade temperature with a normal heart rate, respiratory rate and oxygen saturation — no systemic sepsis physiology. The AI Doctor cannot measure these remotely and infers the absence of toxicity from the patient's lay report.

symptoms.symptom_name: Finger exam: uniform violaceous soft-tissue swelling MCP to DIP with marked tenderness (dactylitis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person exam: uniform tenosynovial swelling of the whole digit is the physical hallmark of dactylitis; the AI Doctor cannot palpate it and relies on the patient's description and the video appearance.

symptoms.symptom_name: Knee exam: moderate fluctuant warm effusion, restricted terminal flexion, stable ligaments; no skin portal of entry

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person exam: a fluctuant warm effusion with stable ligaments and no portal of entry supports an inflammatory effusion; aspiration is still required to exclude infection definitively.
Absence of a skin portal of entry lowers (but does not exclude) the probability of joint sepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Joint aspiration + synovial fluid analysis (cell count, Gram stain, culture, crystals)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Synovial fluid analysis is the definitive test to exclude septic arthritis and crystal disease. It cannot be obtained via telehealth and is arranged as a prompt in-person investigation, not emergently, given the negative acute septic screen.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Psoriatic arthritis (oligoarticular) flare with dactylitis and knee effusion
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of uniform whole-finger dactylitis, nail pitting, simultaneous asymmetrical involvement of two distinct joint types, established psoriasis, no systemic toxicity and a warm but non-spreading, non-progressive effusion identifies a psoriatic spondyloarthritis flare with a NEGATIVE acute septic screen — appropriate for prompt in-person joint assessment and outpatient rheumatology management, not emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person joint examination +/- joint aspiration with synovial fluid analysis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive step to exclude septic arthritis and crystal disease in an acutely hot swollen joint. Cannot be done via telehealth; arrange as a prompt in-person GP/urgent review (within 24-48 hours, same-day if any septic feature appears) — not by emergency ambulance given the negative acute screen.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR) +/- blood cultures if febrile
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support the assessment of inflammatory versus infective activity at the in-person review; blood cultures if a high fever develops.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain radiograph / ultrasound of the affected joints
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Outpatient assessment of erosive/enthesial change and effusion as part of the rheumatology work-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance transfer to ED for the baseline presentation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated as the baseline disposition: the acute septic screen is negative (asymmetrical two-joint pattern, no high fever/rigors, no systemic toxicity, dactylitis and nail pitting on a psoriatic background). Routing every warm psoriatic effusion to an emergency 000 transfer is the over-triage this case is designed to detect — unless a genuine septic trigger (fiery-red spreading hot joint, high fever with rigors, systemic toxicity) appears.
